_description: 课程题目配置 - Maple Town 新内容

questions.instruction: You want an Americano. How do you order?
questions.correct_feedback:
'Can I get + item + please' is the universal ordering sentence!
"Can I get + 东西 + please" —— 万能点单句！
questions.incorrect_feedback:
The polite way to order is 'Can I get an Americano, please?'
礼貌点单用 'Can I get an Americano, please?'

options.label: A
options.text: Can I get an Americano, please?

options.label: C
options.text: I need... that... black coffee thing?

questions.instruction: The barista asks: 'What size? Small, medium, or large?' You want a medium.
questions.correct_feedback:
Small/Medium/Large = 小/中/大. Works at any store!
Small/Medium/Large = 小/中/大，哪家店都用得上。
questions.incorrect_feedback:
Medium means the middle size.
Medium 是中间尺寸。

questions.instruction: The croissant looks great. You want to add one. What do you say?
questions.correct_feedback:
'And a...' is the universal way to add items. Croissant = 可颂🥐
"And a..." 追加点单万能用法。
questions.incorrect_feedback:
Use 'And a croissant, please' to add to your order.
用 'And a croissant, please' 来追加。

questions.instruction: The total is $7.25. 'Cash or card?' You want to pay by card.
questions.correct_feedback:
Card = pay by card, Cash = pay with money.
Card = 刷卡，Cash = 现金。
questions.incorrect_feedback:
To pay by card, say 'Card, please.'
刷卡就说 'Card, please.'

questions.instruction: At American coffee shops, why do they ask for your name?
questions.correct_feedback:
They write your name on the cup to call out when ready. Misspelled names are a universal immigrant experience 😂
名字被写错是全球移民共同经历😂
questions.incorrect_feedback:
They write names on cups to call out orders.
他们写名字是为了叫号。

options.label: A
options.text: To sign you up for membership

options.label: B
options.text: To write on the cup and call your name

questions.instruction: Customer: 'Can I get a latte with oat milk, no sugar?' What does she want?
questions.listening_transcript: Can I get a latte with oat milk, no sugar?

audio_content.speaker: Customer
audio_content.text_cn: 我要一杯拿铁，加燕麦奶，不加糖。
audio_content.text_en: Can I get a latte with oat milk, no sugar?

questions.correct_feedback:
'with oat milk' = add oat milk. 'no sugar' = don't add sugar.
with = 加，no = 不加。
questions.incorrect_feedback:
'with' means add, 'no' means remove.
'with' 表示加，'no' 表示不加。

options.label: A
options.text: Latte with oat milk, no sugar

options.label: B
options.text: Americano with oat milk, no sugar

questions.instruction: Customer: 'Large iced tea. That's it.' What does he want?
questions.correct_feedback:
'That's it' = that's all.
"That's it" = 就这些。
questions.incorrect_feedback:
He said 'Large iced tea'.
他说 'Large iced tea' —— 大杯冰茶。

questions.instruction: A little girl says 'Can I get a hot chocolate? The big one!' What size is 'the big one'?
questions.correct_feedback:
'The big one' is the casual way to say 'Large'.
"The big one" 就是Large的口语说法。
questions.incorrect_feedback:
'Big' = large.
Big = 大。

questions.instruction: 'One cappuccino, one Americano, and two croissants.' How many items total?
questions.correct_feedback:
Don't miss 'two croissants' — 1+1+2 = 4 items!
别漏 'two croissants' —— 1+1+2 = 4样！
questions.incorrect_feedback:
1 cappuccino + 1 Americano + 2 croissants = 4.
1+1+2 = 4样。

questions.instruction: Calculate: Cappuccino $4.50 + Americano $3.75 + 2× Croissant $3.50 = ?
An old lady asks 'What do you recommend?' You want to recommend the Americano.
questions.correct_feedback:
'I recommend...' = 我推荐…… Blogger instinct in English!
"I recommend..." 博主本能！
questions.incorrect_feedback: Use 'I recommend the Americano. It's really good!'

options.label: A
options.text: I recommend the Americano. It's really good!

options.label: C
options.text: I don't know, I just work here.

questions.instruction: Karen: 'I ordered a large latte but got a cappuccino. I want a replacement.' What's her problem?
questions.listening_transcript: I ordered a large latte yesterday, but I got a cappuccino. I want a replacement.

audio_content.speaker: Karen
audio_content.text_cn: 我昨天点了大杯拿铁，但拿到的是卡布奇诺。我要换一杯。
audio_content.text_en: I ordered a large latte yesterday, but I got a cappuccino. I want a replacement.

questions.correct_feedback:
'I ordered A but I got B' — the most common complaint pattern!
"I ordered A but I got B" —— 投诉最常见句式。
questions.incorrect_feedback:
She ordered a latte but received a cappuccino.
她点了拿铁但拿到了卡布奇诺。

questions.instruction: You apologize on behalf of the store. What do you say?
questions.correct_feedback:
'I'm sorry about that' + 'Let me fix it' = Apologize + solve!
道歉+主动解决，万能客诉话术。
questions.incorrect_feedback:
Always apologize first: 'I'm sorry about that. Let me fix it for you.'
先道歉再解决。

options.label: A
options.text: I'm sorry about that. Let me fix it for you.

options.label: C
options.text: Are you sure you didn't order wrong?

questions.instruction: Confirm her order. She wants a large latte with oat milk.
questions.correct_feedback:
'So that's... right?' — professional and reassuring!
"So that's... right?" 确认订单，专业又安心。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Use 'So that's a large latte with oat milk, right?'

options.label: A
options.text: So that's a large latte with oat milk, right?

questions.instruction: Karen asks 'Can I get a refund?' What does 'refund' mean?
questions.correct_feedback:
Refund = 退款. Standard refund request.
Refund = 退款。标准退款说法。
questions.incorrect_feedback:
Refund = getting money back.
Refund 是退钱。

questions.instruction: Tell her she can get a refund.
questions.correct_feedback:
'We can give you a refund' — professional and clear.
"We can give you a refund" 专业又清晰。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Say 'Sure, we can give you a refund.'

options.label: A
options.text: Sure, we can give you a refund.

options.label: C
options.text: You need to call the manager.

questions.instruction: Conflict resolved. Recommend something else while she's happy.
questions.correct_feedback:
'No worries' = 没关系. Recommend while the mood is good!
"No worries" = 没关系。趁心情好推荐——博主本能！
questions.incorrect_feedback: 'No worries! Next time, try the croissant too.' is friendly.

options.label: A
options.text: No worries! Next time, try the croissant too. It's amazing.

options.label: C
options.text: Please don't leave a bad review.

questions.instruction: You want to find milk. How do you ask?
questions.correct_feedback:
'Where can I find...?' — universal store sentence!
"Where can I find...?" 超市找东西万能句。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Use 'Where can I find milk?' — polite and clear.

questions.instruction: Old Chen: 'Aisle 3, on your left.' Where is the milk?
questions.correct_feedback:
Aisle = store row. 'On your left' = left side.
Aisle = 货架排。"On your left" = 左边。
questions.incorrect_feedback:
Aisle 3, left side.
第3排，左边。

questions.instruction: You want the cheapest milk. Whole $3.99, Low-fat $3.79, Oat $5.49. Which one?
questions.correct_feedback:
Low-fat $3.79 is cheapest. Reading price tags = survival skill!
Low-fat $3.79最便宜。看价签是生存技能！
questions.incorrect_feedback: $3.79 < $3.99 < $5.49. Low-fat is cheapest.

questions.instruction: You see a sign that says 'SALE'. What does it mean?
questions.correct_feedback:
SALE = on sale / discount. Bargains ahead!
SALE = 打折促销。有便宜捡！
questions.incorrect_feedback:
SALE means discounted, not sold out.
SALE 是打折，不是售完。

questions.instruction: Old Chen: 'Did you find everything okay?' What is he asking?
questions.correct_feedback:
'Did you find everything okay?' — standard American cashier greeting. Reply 'Yes, thanks!'
美国收银员标准开场白。回答 'Yes, thanks!'
questions.incorrect_feedback: He's asking if you found everything you needed.

options.label: C
options.text: Asking if you want a bag

questions.instruction: You can't find eggs. How do you ask?
questions.correct_feedback:
Review! 'Where can I find + item' works every time.
复习！"Where can I find + 东西" 每次都管用。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Use 'Where can I find eggs?'

questions.instruction: Old Chen: 'Aisle 1, next to the bread.' Where are the eggs?
questions.correct_feedback:
'next to' = beside. Eggs are in Aisle 1, next to bread.
"next to" = 旁边。
questions.incorrect_feedback: 'Next to the bread' means right beside the bread.

questions.instruction: Two types of eggs: Free-range (6) $4.99, Regular (12) $3.49. You want the cheaper one.
questions.correct_feedback:
Regular = normal. Free-range = free-roaming (expensive).
Regular = 普通。Free-range = 散养（贵）。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Regular Eggs $3.49 < Free-range $4.99.

questions.instruction: Three bottles look similar: Olive Oil, Sesame Oil, Vegetable Oil. You need sesame oil.
questions.correct_feedback:
Sesame = 芝麻. Read labels correctly!
Sesame = 芝麻。读懂标签不会买错！
questions.incorrect_feedback: Sesame Oil = 芝麻油。

questions.instruction: Old Chen asks 'You want a bag?' You want one.
questions.correct_feedback:
'Yes, please.' Simple!
"Yes, please." 简单搞定。
questions.incorrect_feedback: Just say 'Yes, please.'

questions.instruction: Old Chen gives you hot sauce and says 'On the house.' What does that mean?
questions.correct_feedback:
'On the house' = free / store's treat. Old Chen is tough outside, soft inside!
"On the house" = 免费送。老陈嘴硬心软！
questions.incorrect_feedback: 'On the house' means free of charge.
